marc:datafield.marc:subfield: Algeria, Bangladesh, Benin, Congo, Cuba, Egypt, Ethiopia, India, Jamaica, Jordan, Lao People's Democratic Republic, Libyan Arab Jamahiriya, Sierra Leone, Syrian Arab Republic, United Republic of Tanzania ,Viet Nam, Yugoslavia and Zambia.

Additional sponsors: Afghanistan, Angola, Bulgaria, Burundi, Byelorussian Soviet Socialist Republic, Czechoslovakia, Democratic Yemen, German Democratic Republic, Ghana, Guinea, Guyana, Hungary, Madagascar, Mauritania, Mongolia, Niger, Nigeria, Pakistan, Senegal, Somalia, Sudan, Togo and Ukrainian Soviet Socialist Republic.
Policies of apartheid of the Government of South Africa.
93rd plenary meeting, Wednesday, 24 January 1979, New York

In: Official records of the General Assembly, 33rd session, plenary meetings, Vol. III (of 3 volumes) : verbatim records of the 77th to 108th meetings, 11-21 December 1978, 15-29 January 1979 and 23-31 May 1979 - UNA(01)/R3 - p. 1629-1643.
UN TRUST FUND FOR SOUTH AFRICA
UN Trust Fund for South Africa
S-G. Note transmitting text of para. 13 of GA res. 33/182 A
Transmits para. 13 of GA res. 33/182 A concerning Namibia question.
S-G. Note transmitting the text of paras. 7, 9, 10 and 11 of GA res. 33/38 B
Transmits the text of paras. 7, 9, 10 and 11 of GA res. 33/38 B concerning the Southern Rhodesia question.
SC Committee Established in Pursuance of res. 253 (1968) concerning the Question of Southern Rhodesia. 11th report
11th report of the Security Council Committee Established in Pursuance of Resolution 253 (1968) concerning the Question of Southern Rhodesia.
SCOR, 34th year, Spec. suppl. no. 2, Vol. I
Appendix II of Chap. II contains excerpt from Report on the supply of petroleum and petroleum products to Rhodesia by T.H. Bingham and S.M. Gray.
UN. Security Council Committee Established in pursuance of Resolution 253 (1968) concerning the Question of Southern Rhodesia
UN. Security Council Committee Established in pursuance of Resolution 253 (1968) concerning the Question of Southern Rhodesia
S-G. Note including summary of measures against South Africa requested by the GA at its 33rd sess.
Reproduces operative paras. of General Assembly resolution 33/183 A to O concerning measures requested by the General Assembly against South Africa.
Question concerning the situation in Southern Rhodesia.
Question concerning the situation in Southern Rhodesia.
Question concerning the situation in Southern Rhodesia.
Complaint by Angola against South Africa.
Pan Africanist Congress of Azania (South Africa)
Complaint by Angola against South Africa.
Security Council resolution 445 (1979) [condemning Southern Rhodesia's armed invasions of Angola, Mozambique and Zambia]
adopted by the Security Council at its 2122nd meeting, on 8 March 1979.
Condemns Southern Rhodesia's armed invasions of Angola, Mozambique and Zambia.
In: Resolutions and decisions of the Security Council, 1979. - S/INF/35. - 1980. - p. 13-14. - (SCOR, 34th year).
Security Council resolution 447 (1979) [on South Africa's policies towards Angola and other front-line States]
adopted by the Security Council at its 2139th meeting, on 28 March 1979.
Condemns South Africa's armed invasions of Angola.
In: Resolutions and decisions of the Security Council, 1979. - S/INF/35. - 1980. - p. 17-18. - (SCOR, 34th year).
Charter of the United Nations (1945). Chapter VII
working paper prepared by the Secretariat.
Report of the World Conference to Combat Racism and Racial Discrimination, Geneva, 14-25 August 1978
Declaration on Race and Racial Prejudice (1978)
World Conference to Combat Racism and Racial Discrimination (1978 : Geneva)
Note verbale dated 8 February 1979 from the Permanent Representative of Portugal to the United Nations addressed to the Secretary-General.
Concerns implementation of Security Council resolution 409(1979) imposing further sanctions against Southern Rhodesia.

Letter dated 23 March 1979 from the Chairman of the Security Council Committee established in pursuance of resolution 253 (1968) concerning the question of Southern Rhodesia addressed to the President of the Security Council.
Transmits interim report concerning Southern Rhodesia question.
UN. Security Council Committee Established in pursuance of Resolution 253 (1968) concerning the Question of Southern Rhodesia. Chair
Bangladesh, Bolivia, Gabon, Jamaica, Kuwait, Nigeria, Zambia.
Security Council resolution 448 (1979) [reaffirming the April 1979 elections and the results thereof in Southern Rhodesia to be null and void]
adopted by the Security Council at its 2143rd meeting, on 30 April 1979.
Reaffirms the April 1979 elections and the results thereof in Southern Rhodesia to be null and void.
In: Resolutions and decisions of the Security Council, 1979. - S/INF/35. - 1980. - p. 15. - (SCOR, 34th year).

Letter dated 31 January 1979 from the Permanent Representative of Spain to the United Nations addressed to the Chairman of the Security Council Committee Established by Resolution 421 (1977) concerning the Question of South Africa.
Special reports of the Special Committee against Apartheid
Previously circulated under the symbols A/33/22/Add.1-S/12858/Add.1 and A/33/22/Add.2-S/12858/Add.2.
1st special report : oil sanctions against South Africa -- 2nd special report : recent developments in relations between Israel and South Africa.
Report of the Special Committee against Apartheid

Report of the Security Council Committee Established in Pursuance of Resolution 253 (1968) concerning the Question of Southern Rhodesia in implementation of paragraphs 3, 12 and 8 of Security Council resolutions 409 (1977), 411 (1977) and 445 (1979), respectively.
Contains Committee recommendations on sanctions against Southern Rhodesia.

Letter dated 1 August 1979 from the Permanent Representative of Spain to the United Nations addressed to the Chairman of the Security Council Committee Established by Resolution 421 (1977) concerning the Question of South Africa.
Concerns arms embargo against South Africa.

Implementation of the Declaration on the Granting of Independence to Colonial Countries and Peoples.

Activities of foreign economic and other interests which are impeding the implementation of the Declaration on the Granting of Independence to Colonial Countries and Peoples in Southern Rhodesia and Namibia and in all other Territories under colonial domination and efforts to eliminate colonialism, apartheid and racial discrimination in southern Africa :
Resolution adopted by the Special Committee at its 1154th meeting on August 1979.

Activities of foreign economic and other interests which are impeding the implementation of the Declaration on the Granting of Independence to Colonial Countries and Peoples in Namibia and in all other Territories under colonial domination and efforts to eliminate colonialism, apartheid and racial discrimination in southern Africa :
Algeria, Angola, Congo, Ethiopia, Guinea, Guinea-Bissau, Madagascar, Mozambique, Sao Tome and Principe, Senegal, Seychelles, Zambia and Zimbabwe

Activities of foreign economic and other interests which are impeding the implementation of the Declaration on the Granting of Independence to Colonial Countries and Peoples in Namibia and in all other Territories under colonial domination and efforts to eliminate colonialism, apartheid and racial discrimination in southern Africa.

Afghanistan, Algeria, Angola, Bahrain, Barbados, Benin, Chad, Congo, Cuba, Democratic Yemen, Egypt, Ethiopia, Gambia, Ghana, Grenada, Guinea, Guinea-Bissau, Guyana, India, Kenya, Kuwait, Lao People's Democratic Republic, Liberia, Libyan Arab Jamahiriya, Madagascar, Malaysia, Mali, Mauritania, Mozambique, Nicaragua, Niger, Nigeria, Pakistan, Panama, Sao Tome and Principe, Seychelles, Sierra Leone, Somalia, Sudan, Syrian Arab Republic, Tunisia, Uganda, United Republic of Cameroon, United Republic of Tanzania, Viet Nam, Yugoslavia and Zambia.
Policies of apartheid of the Government of South Africa.
International Conference on Sanctions against South Africa :

Afghanistan, Algeria, Angola, Bahrain, Barbados, Benin, Chad, Comoros, Congo, Cuba, Democratic Yemen, Egypt, Ethiopia, Gambia, Ghana, Grenada, Guinea, Guinea-Bissau, Guyana, India, Kenya, Kuwait, Lao People's Democratic Republic, Liberia, Libyan Arab Jamahiriya, Madagascar, Malaysia, Mali, Mauritania, Mozambique, Nicaragua, Niger, Nigeria, Pakistan, Panama, Sao Tome and Principe, Seychelles, Sierra Leone, Somalia, Sudan, Suriname, Syrian Arab Republic, Trinidad and Tobago, Tunisia, Uganda, United Republic of Cameroon, United Republic of Tanzania, Viet Nam and Zambia.
Policies of apartheid of the Government of South Africa.

Activities of foreign economic and other interests which are impeding the implementation of the Declaration on the Granting of Independence to Colonial Countries and Peoples in Namibia and in all other Territories under colonial domination and efforts to eliminate colonialism, apartheid and racial discrimination in southern Africa :#resolution

Activities of foreign economic anf other interests which are impeding the implementation of the Declaration on the Granting of Independence to Colonial Countries and Peoples in Namibia and in all other Territories under colonial domination and efforts to eliminate colonialism, apartheid and racial discrimination in southern Africa.
Adopted at the 57th plenary meeting, 11 Nov. 1980.

In: Resolutions and decisions adopted by the General Assembly during its 35th session, 16 September-17 December 1980, 15 and 16 January, 2-6 March and 11 May 1981. - A/35/48. - 1981. - p. 220-222. - (GAOR, 35th sess., Suppl. no. 48).

Activities of foreign economic and other interests which are impeding the implementation of the Declaration on the Granting of Independence to Colonial Countries and Peoples in Namibia and in all other Territories under colonial domination and efforts to eliminate colonialism, apartheid and racial discrimination in southern Africa.

Administrative and financial implications of the draft resolutions contained in documents A/35/L.13, A/35/L.14, A/35/L.15, A/35/L.16, A/35/L.17, A/35/L.18, A/35/L.19, A/35/L.21, A/35/L.22, A/35/L.23, A/35/L.25, A/35/L.26, A/35/L.27 and A/35/L.28 :
submitted by the Secretary-General in accordance with rule 153 of the rules of procedure of the General Assembly.
Policies of apartheid of the Government of South Africa.

Adverse consequences for the enjoyment of human rights of political, military, economic and other forms of assistance given to colonial and racist regimes in southern Africa : report of the 3rd Committee : General Assembly, 35th session
UN. Special Rapporteur on Adverse Consequences for the Enjoyment of Human Rights of Political, Military, Economic and Other Forms of Assistance Given to Colonial and Racist Regimes in Southern Africa
Adverse consequences for the enjoyment of human rights of political, military, economic and other forms of assistance given to colonial and racist regimes in southern Africa.
Administrative and financial implications of the draft resolutions A/35/L.13 to A/35/L.16/Rev.1, A/35/L.17 to A/35/L.19, A/35/L.21 to A/35/L.23/Rev.1 and A/35/L.25 to A/35/L.28 :
Policies of apartheid of the Government of South Africa.
